seasonality: Fog and wind can affect waterfront comfort, so keep layers and indoor food stops available.
Clear days support longer Embarcadero walks and bay-view photo stops.
transport_advice: Keep waterfront landmarks grouped to avoid repeated hill climbs and cross-city transfers.
Use transit, walking, or short rideshare hops between the Ferry Building, Embarcadero, Fisherman's Wharf, and nearby viewpoints.
planning_tips: A waterfront day should balance landmarks, bay views, and food breaks rather than overloading distant neighborhoods.
Pair Ferry Building or Fisherman's Wharf with nearby waterfront walking and one optional viewpoint.
Avoid putting Golden Gate Park and the waterfront into the same short half-day unless the user accepts more transit time.
title: San Francisco Waterfront, Landmarks, and Bay Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for San Francisco waterfront routes: the Ferry Building, Embarcadero, Fisherman's Wharf, Alcatraz context, and bay viewpoints work best as a grouped waterfront plan. The itinerary should respect fog, wind, hills, and transfer time.

district: Golden Gate Park, Presidio, Twin Peaks
seasonality: Fog can obscure bridge and hilltop views, so keep timing flexible for viewpoint stops.
Rainy days should shorten exposed park walks and add nearby indoor or cafe breaks.
transport_advice: Group Presidio, Crissy Field, and bridge viewpoints together rather than crossing back to the waterfront repeatedly.
Public transit is workable but schedules should leave buffer time for hill and park transfers.
planning_tips: For parks and view requests, choose either a Golden Gate/Presidio cluster or a Golden Gate Park cluster before adding distant stops.
Keep photo stops shorter on foggy or windy days and use nearby cafes or museums as backups.
Avoid overloading the day with both western parks and northeastern waterfront landmarks unless the traveler accepts extra transit.
title: San Francisco Parks, Bridge Views, and Outdoor Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for San Francisco outdoor routes: Golden Gate Bridge viewpoints, the Presidio, Golden Gate Park, Crissy Field, and Twin Peaks require weather flexibility and careful clustering. Strong itineraries reduce cross-city transit and keep fog or wind backups available.

district: Mission, Chinatown, North Beach, Ferry Building
seasonality: Rain or strong wind makes steep streets harder, so keep routes compact and near transit.
Sunny afternoons work well for Mission murals, Dolores Park, or North Beach walking.
transport_advice: Food and neighborhood walks should stay compact because hills and transfers can slow the day.
Use BART, Muni, or short rideshare segments to start or end neighborhood routes near the hotel.
planning_tips: For food and culture requests, build around one or two adjacent neighborhoods such as Chinatown and North Beach or the Mission.
Pair meal stops with murals, bookstores, markets, or short park breaks when nearby.
Avoid mixing a full Mission food route with a full waterfront landmark route in the same half-day.
title: San Francisco Food, Neighborhoods, and Culture Walk Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for San Francisco food routes: compact neighborhood planning is essential because hills, transit transfers, and microclimates affect pacing. The Mission, Chinatown, North Beach, Ferry Building, and Hayes Valley can each support focused food and culture walks.
